data.body:
## Description

**What**: Verify that a project created via the template onboarding path passes validate.sh --ci end-to-end.
**Why**: This is the ultimate integration test — proving that template installation + DSO infrastructure setup produces a valid, working project.
**Scope**:
- IN: Run validate.sh --ci on a project created via each template path (NextJS, Flask, Rails via nava-platform; Jekyll via git clone). Fix any configuration collisions where validate.sh picks up template config instead of host project config. Ensure the validation runs against the correct dso-config.conf.
- OUT: Template installation logic. Registry parsing. Individual install path error handling.

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, validate.sh --ci passes on a project created via the nava-platform template path for at least one template (NextJS)
  ← Satisfies: "After install + Phase 3, validate.sh --ci passes"
- When this story is complete, validate.sh --ci passes on a project created via the Jekyll git clone path
  ← Satisfies: "After install + Phase 3, validate.sh --ci passes"
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] validate.sh may pick up template's own .claude/dso-config.conf — verify config resolution order.
- [Reliability] Templates may include CI workflow files that conflict with DSO's generated workflows.
